reviews.review_msg: Two courses with Paul before he retired. Best: Paul picks good reads for the course! You go to Paul's course not thinking about the grade. You go to it because you know that English studies are about interdisciplinary and critical disposition and tons of reading. Honours effort, ideas and passion for the text!

He cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He goes beyond the typical teaching roles of a professor and tries to make real, personal connections with all of his students. What I liked the most from his class is that he encouraged us to be creative. As someone who dislikes formal essay writing, I enjoyed doing the assignments.

Paul is very caring, passionate, and inspirational. He is not the conventional professor; of course he wants you to develop into a good student, but he also wants you to develop into a kind person. He allows for maximum creativity and encourages you to take risks in order to learn something about yourself. Highly recommend.

SUCH A GOOD GUY!!!! I love him, he's so kind, caring, and intelligent. He is very open minded, and is always willing to lend a helping hand. He's truly one of the best profs that SFU has and I wish I could take more classes with him! Highly highly recommend!

Paul is a genuinely one of the sweetest professors I've had. He cares deeply about teaching and allows for students to explore literary works through creative projects instead of essays. He gives detailed feedback on graded work. He is a fair marker, and genuinely cares about seeing his students succeed. Take a class with him before he retires!!

One of the only professors who has the ability to leave an everlasting impression. He creates a safe learning environment. He allows you to truly be yourself & that in itself is refreshing. The students who are commenting on his "pointless" stories clearly did not understand that each anecdote served a purpose and related to the lecture. He's a gem

Paul is a great teacher! He is willing to put in the work if you are. He will provide you feedback on your essays and builds an atmosphere to promote teaching opposed to the standard "getting through the curriculum" many teachers offer.  English was never my strong suit, but Paul made me feel confident to continue writing. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.

After taking this course, I became a huge fan of him. His lectures may come off as radical at times but his ideas are very inspirational and thought-provoking. And you can literally see he's really passionate about teaching and don't be afraid to take advantage of his office hours. He's very accommodating and provides in-depth feedback.

Paul is passionate and genuinely seems to enjoy teaching. He is helpful a sense that he offers extensive feedback on essays and assignments to help you do better next time. He is also an easy marker, oh, and there's no need to attend every class, participation matters but you'll get  a decent mark without going to every class.

Paul is eclectic. His passion for the topic is clear. For students however, the material is difficult and complicated to process, let alone write about. Little room for personal interpretation considering it's his lectures, reading his textbook. Very helpful nonetheless. TA's are not willing to mark easier, but still use them for guidance.

Paul is an amazing professor. He deeply cares about his students and provides students with the best feedback and help. Paul is highly intelligent, passionate, kind and a great lecturer. What makes Paul so unique is that he allows his students to demonstrate their learning in a creative way (creative writing instead of essay). Best prof at SFU!

Paul is a very boring teacher. His lectures are as interesting as eating a whole dry stale hamburger bun. Not only does he not talk about the course material in lectures he does not seem to know the material for the Pros Genres class. Would not take again.

His lectures are very off topic and communicate little to no information that is of any relevance to the course. If you enjoy a prof who rambles, reads directly from the book, and has troubles organizing the entire lecture than you've chosen the right course.

Paul is the best professor you could possibly have if you have a very creative mind. He is open to different mediums of academia and he has an appreciation for them. His dedication to his students is so touching and we all appreciated how understand or compassionate he was towards time management. He is just amazing.

The lectures were almost completely off-topic (literally). We talked about the professor's personal life about as much as we did the course novels. However, he's a very nice, accommodating professor & I would definitely take another class with him again.
Poor quality.

Very nice man, the only thing is that his grading is not on the easy side, but it's not unfair either. He's VERY open to creativity, so you can make prior arrangements for your papers, and instead make the paper into a project. Very understandable, and easy going.

This man was neither funny nor interesting. And his class was absolutely not informative. I would avoid him unless you A) Have no personal opinions or advanced knowledge concerning life and/or literature. B) Enjoy rambling, pointless lectures and lots of useless anecdotes. Oh, or C) Want a four-credit nap time.

Haha this class was sort of a joke. But St. Pierre was great. Even though the class was absolutely not mandatory.. I found myself wanting to attend just to see what he would come up with next. Very interesting course content. I loved learning about it. But I found the tutorials way more helpful than his lectures. But he was alright!
Paul is a great professor. Interesting course and I would recommend any course with this prof! Going to class was not mandatory as everything he showed was on the class website

Love this guy, shows tons of youtube videos in lecture and the whole semester is really easy going with him. Never picked up the textbook once for this class and ended up with a A-. Just do well on both assignments and your set. BTW no midterm!
Such a nice guy, love him!

I love this class and this professor! I only chose this class because there were literally no other options open, but I am *so* glad I did. Interesting topics, eclectic way of teaching, unconventional assignments. You will never have a professor like Professor St Pierre, and no I'm not his hired PR, haha. If you can, take him!
Interesting course. Very different. Paul's a good guy that is very willing to help, and always accepts creativity and new ideas. Recommend this class will him as the prof for sure.

One of the best English profs I've ever had in my life. I hated English and it was my 2nd language, but he spent extra time with me improving my writing skills and told me how to selectively read his assigned readings so that I won't die. Curved my final exam even though I didn't finish everything in the allotted time.

An interesting character, which makes him an interesting lecturer. Discussions went weird and wonderfully places which took sometimes very obscure material and made it relatable. Some of his ideas seem bizarre, but if you pay attention, they actually tend to help overall comprehension. Definitely would recommend this course.
Paul is what I imagine God would be like in human form. Enough said. Take his class. Learn and be inspired.

Brilliant. Quiet, thoughtful, gracious, generous -in short, an outstanding human being. I hope that someday I can repay him somehow. Many of the reviews here will describe him as quirky. He is, but that misses the point. Pay attention to how carefully & artfully constructed his lectures are. He cares more about lit and life than others: revere him.

Paul was my instructor in his first year at SFU.  He taught the obligatory Commonwealth Lit and Can Lit that none of the American teachers wanted to teach.  Paul was fantastic then, and full of new ideas and interest.  It sounds like he is still teaching fresh ideas and giving those amazing critiques to assignments.

Paul's chosen course material (especially the final book, Gender Trouble) was far too advanced for a first year class. A book like Gender Trouble is a 3rd year book (at minimum) and should not be given to first years, especially in first year W course where a good number of students may not even have English as their first language. nice guy though

Paul is a wonderful person.Is SO approachable,caring completely for student needs&concerns.Lectures are sometimes rambly&attendance isnt mandatory,but still worth going to.Gives exceptional feedback to students on papers and presentations returning them promptly w/pagelong helpful comments and praises.Always very willing to help.Highly Recommended!

Paul is an incredible person. It is through his heart and approachability that you are able to get the most out of his classes. I am so very thankful that he is a professor at SFU. He inspires me to think outside the box and be more than typical. Awesome professor, I am grateful for all I have gained by being in his class.
Not only is he a good teacher, but he has a really good heart towards his students. He is very caring, understanding, and super sweet to all of his students. He is also a very good listener
Very unique and bubbly. Eccentric even. Great teacher, so interesting and so intelligent
one of the most memorable profs from my academic career. Also one of the most eccentric characters of the SFU faculty.

he is my favourite prof of all time.  besides being a nice man, he is incredibly intelligent and passionate about his subject matter.  for papers be as creative as you can, he loves anything that is different and offbeat and if you can include some references to pop culture you are good as gold.

I love this prof and this class so much I am taking another class with him next term! Admittedly, he is eccentric, but is that really a bad thing? He knows his material and he is passionate about it. He is also more than fair in his grading and his comments on returned assignments couldn't be any more helpful.
Paul is an engaging and articulate speaker with obvious interest in the course material. Great.

This professor likes to look sweet. However, he acts weird and projects nervousness that is unnerving and unsettling to be a part of. He tends to criticize the English department quite vocally and yet he manages to remain very much one of the "establishment".  One wonders how he manages this dual identity.  He definately lacks discretion.

I'm worried by some of the comments on here -- I guess lots of people don't know genius when they see it!  If you guys wants stats and clear, precise knowledge, go take a business class!  St. Pierre is fantastic!  He's sweet, insightful, and "shows" you the themes of the class, instead of teaching them "at" you.

OMG. PAUL IS THE BEST! He is absolutely intelligent and articulate. He is also very sweet, caring and kind-hearted. He cares about students as actual people rather than numbers in a class. Most of the stories and books that he chose were very interesting and enlightening. I highly recommend his classes. SFU should be proud of having Paul SERIOUSLY!

He's Sweet, articulate, inspirational, and very well prepared for class.  He's very shy, but he gets over it once he gets going. Subject material is amazing.  His quiet ways are very endearing. I would take his class again and again. And I love his personal anecdotes!
Paul is smart but very strange.
Sincere and works hard to make the learning experience relevent to each student's individual interests.  A unique and healthy sense of humour.

The course topic drifted.  It was all about him rather than all about criticism.  Classes had no apparent agenda, rhyme or reason.  Naively gave the floor each class to the verbose duplicitious panderess of class.

I've never seen a man look uncomfortable for three straight months. If you had to take odds on which prof would show up to class nude, holding a flyswatter and crying incessantly, St. Pierre would be your heavy favourite.

Defines the word eccentric. Has a nervous sweatiness that is difficult to watch.  Lectures are boring, vague, obscure. Gives many personal anecdotes, half of which he admits are not true, and furthermore fails to relate them in any coherent way to the subject matter. But he IS an easy marker!
extraordinary teacher and artist! enacts his pedagogy, dignifies literary works in question as well as his students. engaging. "seasoned," one could say. privilege to have studied with him.

Picked favorites at the start of the term. Prefers students already versant in lit crit. jargon. Unhelpful when approached for individual help.  Seemed to begrudge the "A" my term paper earned.  My success as a PhD is in spite of him, not becaus
he is great!!! the course is easy, with easy marking, easy final and the stories are pretty good!!
He looks like a cross between woody allen and john cleese.  He's a nice guy and teaches interesting classes.
Easy class.. weird stories... easy marking.. easy course load what more do u want?

The only reason to come to his lectures are his truly bizzare comments; if the man didn't have tenure, I don't think he'd survive. He's quite fair with his marking and assignments, though. Woody Allen lookalike.

Bizarro.  Spent half the semester speaking on the trials and tribulations of obtaining good office space within the English faculty.  Wrote a book and referenced it every 3.5 seconds in class.  Which, of course, had zero to do with Plato/critical theory.
One of the worst classes I've ever had.

stories on picking out urinated toilet paper out of the urinal as a hobbby... just another day in St. Pierre's lecture (most definitely preparing me for the real world). Hey if you want a lecture you wont be worried about missing this is the class for you

Yeesh--this guy freaked me out! Talks way too much about downright weird and irrelevant stuff. I mean, I REALLY didn't need to know about his sexual exploits all over Europe. Lectures didn't help me much.
What a great educator. He was always supportive and constructive in his comments. It is because of you that I decided to follow my heart and become an English teacher. Thanks Paul.(:
paul is awesome...really funny and totally knows his stuff.  hes really nice and genuinely cares about his students.  take a class with him if you can.

Very good teacher, often can grasp things that you would never have thought of. Truly cares about helping students, (any teacher that allows you to call him at home does). For ENGL101 final, he pretty much gave us the whole exam before we had to write it.

Paul is an intelligent man and a fair marker. He actually looks forward to speaking with students on an individual basis. One question...How does everything in literature relate to Barry Humphries/Dame Edna? Take his class to find out!

Very inspiring and intellegent man. Relates lecture material to his childhood and publications, all of which are very interesting. Sometimes says bizarre things in class that cracks everyone up. Seems to really care for his students
I did not like him at all. His lectures made no sense and he spent have the time talking about his personal life and his publications. Very weird sense of humour.
He's a funny guy. Weird humour and very inspiring. Very nice.
'Peter'is a great human being, but needs to learn how to be more selective about what he says out loud before he could ever be a great teacher. The guy needs to get over Dame Edna!
ya, he's a crazy man. once mimed his entire lecture, white gloves and all. but we need a little more of that, no? post-colonial expert.
stuck in the idiom of acadamia this prof is really all about teaching.

Has a huge thing for post-colonialism... quite eccentric, fair marker, relatively easy course... likes to talk about his personal life and his own publishment a lot. has a sense of humour, occasionally does extremely bizarre things in lecture. A cool prof
No Comments
a wonderful wonderful wonderful man...and a great inspiration
No Comments
spent more time talking about uninteresting personal life than literature
Intersting intellegent AND Down to earth
coolest male feminist i've ever known - a great prof and an inspiration
you never know what he'll say next
knows his stuff. is very unorthodox. i like it!
kind, a renaissance man, honourable and fair
awesome prof, esp on halloween hehe last semester
Inspirational, spiritual, fair, respectful and remarkable!
